fields.name: National Emergency
fields.description: All emergencies — police, fire, ambulance

fields.name: Police
fields.description: Law enforcement & road accidents

fields.name: Ambulance
fields.description: Medical emergency & accident victims

fields.name: Fire Brigade
fields.description: Fire emergencies on roads

fields.name: Road Accident Relief
fields.description: National Highway accident helpline

fields.name: Women Helpline
fields.description: Safety helpline for women

fields.title: Always wear a seatbelt
fields.description: Seatbelts reduce the risk of death in accidents by 45%. All occupants including rear passengers must wear seatbelts at all times.

fields.title: No use of mobile phone while driving
fields.description: Using a mobile phone while driving increases the risk of accidents by 4 times. Use hands-free devices or pull over to make calls.

fields.title: Do not drink and drive
fields.description: Driving under the influence of alcohol or drugs is illegal and extremely dangerous. Blood Alcohol Concentration (BAC) limit is 30mg per 100ml of blood in India.

fields.title: Follow speed limits
fields.description: Speed limits are set for safety. Urban roads: 50 km/h, Highways: 100 km/h for cars. Overspeeding is a leading cause of road fatalities.

fields.title: Use zebra crossings
fields.description: Pedestrians must use designated zebra crossings and foot overbridges. Always wait for the green pedestrian signal before crossing.

fields.title: Cyclists must use cycle lanes
fields.description: Cyclists should use designated cycle tracks where available. Always ride on the left side of the road and use hand signals when turning.

fields.title: Maintain safe following distance
fields.description: Keep a safe distance from the vehicle ahead. The 3-second rule — pick a fixed point, and ensure at least 3 seconds pass before you reach it after the vehicle ahead.

fields.title: Give way to emergency vehicles
fields.description: Always move to the left and stop when you hear a siren. Blocking emergency vehicles is a punishable offence.

fields.name: Stop Sign
fields.description: Come to a complete halt before the stop line. Proceed only when safe.

fields.name: No Entry
fields.description: Vehicles are prohibited from entering this road or zone.

fields.name: Speed Limit
fields.description: Do not exceed the indicated speed limit in this zone.

fields.name: Sharp Curve Ahead
fields.description: A sharp bend is ahead. Reduce speed and stay in your lane.

fields.name: School Zone
fields.description: Children may be crossing. Drive slowly and carefully.

fields.name: Hospital Ahead
fields.description: A hospital is nearby. Avoid honking and drive carefully.

fields.name: Parking
fields.description: Parking is permitted in this area.

fields.name: Pedestrian Crossing
fields.description: Pedestrians have right of way. Stop for them to cross.

fields.tip: Always check your mirrors before changing lanes or overtaking.
Never drive when drowsy. Take a break every 2 hours on long trips.
Keep your headlights on during rain and fog for better visibility.
Always wear a helmet — it reduces the risk of fatal head injury by 69%.
Wear reflective gear when riding at night to be visible to other drivers.
Never ride between two large vehicles — you may be in a blind spot.
Always look both ways before crossing, even on a one-way street.
Walk on the footpath or extreme right side of the road facing oncoming traffic.
Avoid using your phone or earphones while walking on or near roads.
